title: strange behavior from podman images
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
Filling podman images for local user and then moving this cache 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull $IMG
2. mv /home/$USER/.local/share/containers $SOMEWHERE

3. podman --root $SOMEWHERE images 
doesn't show anything.

Using the additionalimagestores option in storage.conf has the same problem.

**Describe the results you received:**
REPOSITORY                 TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE

**Describe the results you expected:**
REPOSITORY                 TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE
$IMG latest  018c9d7b792b  2 weeks ago  1.45 MB

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:      2.0.4
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.6
Git Commit:   cab27868766a3b01ac14ff46180af8644c41d1cd
Built:        Fri Jul 31 22:31:25 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (output of `pacman -Qs`):**

```
[user@test user]# pacman -Qs podman
local/podman 2.0.4-1
    Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
I'm using podman in docker, but I'm pretty sure it doesn't matter much.

comments.body: Could you kill the podman that is running in your user session if there is one?

Ok I just got this to work, but it was not pretty.  The --root directory needs to point at the storage directory.

```
$ mv .local/share/containers/ ~/mycontainers
$ ./bin/podman --root ~/mycontainers/storage images
Error: database libpod root directory (staticdir) "/home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" does not match our libpod root directory (staticdir) "/home/dwalsh/mycontainers/storage/libpod": database configuration mismatch
```
The problem is libpod has hard coded information about paths into the database. So I remove the libpod database.
```
rm ~/mycontainers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
./bin/podman --root ~/mycontainers/storage images | head -3
REPOSITORY                         TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
localhost/fedora-user              latest  03427907569a  16 hours ago  189 MB
localhost/testimg                  latest  60c1e2cbaa49  2 days ago    5.85 MB
```
Note removing the boltdb will cause your system to loose track of the containers.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I explained how to do it, I am closing.
